definition: Freedom from greed, expressed as non-grasping and openness rather than acquisitive pull.
notes: The project keeps non-greed as the default because it preserves the lexical relation to lobha. Generosity can work in explanatory prose when the positive lived expression is in view, but it is narrower than the doctrinal category itself. The term belongs inside the wholesome-root framework and should not be reduced only to giving material things.

context_rules.context: In prose emphasizing positive expression in conduct
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the practical manifestation is central.

context_rules.context: In prose stressing freedom from grasping
context_rules.notes: Useful when the contrast with greed needs to feel more immediate.

context_rules.context: When the passage is naming one of the wholesome roots
context_rules.notes: Keep the lexical symmetry with lobha visible.

example_phrases.translation: non-greed is a root of the wholesome
